failedAssertions.reason: C dispatch causes SIGSEGV (exit code 139). Combined benchmark cannot complete. Additionally, throughput severely degraded (11.42 tok/s vs expected 38+ tok/s).
Cannot run correctness validation due to C dispatch segfault.

Sprint 5 baseline showed severely degraded throughput: 11.42 tok/s (expected 38+ tok/s). Previous round showed 40.06 tok/s. Throughput degradation may be due to GPU thermal throttling or system state.
Long-generation stability test (500 steps) timed out after 600 seconds due to severely degraded throughput (~11 tok/s).

appliedUpdates.description: Documented C dispatch segfault issue and recommended testing without C dispatch

test_simple_decode.py.evidence: Basic decode without C dispatch works. Kernel P2P enabled.

test_kernel_p2p.py.evidence: Kernel P2P without C dispatch works.

test_c_dispatch.py.evidence: C dispatch causes segfault when enabled.

test_c_dispatch_no_p2p.py.evidence: C dispatch causes segfault even without kernel P2P.

test_final_validation.py.evidence: Progressive fallback passed (3 modes). Sprint 5 baseline showed degraded throughput (11.42 tok/s). Long-generation test timed out.

throughputResults.root_cause_hypothesis: GPU thermal throttling or system state degradation

recommendations: CRITICAL: Investigate C dispatch segfault - root cause needed before any combined optimization testing
CRITICAL: Investigate throughput degradation - 11.42 tok/s vs expected 38+ tok/s suggests system-level issue
Consider rebooting dev server or resetting GPU state before next validation round
Test with fresh Docker container to rule out memory fragmentation

frictions.description: C dispatch segfault prevents combined optimization testing
Throughput severely degraded vs previous round
Long-running tests timeout due to slow throughput

blockers.suggestedFix: Debug C dispatch memory access patterns, check for null pointer dereferences in c_dispatch.c
Reboot dev server, check GPU thermal state, verify no other processes using GPUs
